telegramText: MARKET WRAP — Apr 24, 2026 Markets shrugged off Middle East naval escalation and energy disruptions, with US equities hitting fresh highs as investors bet on American resilience over global fragility. The risk-on rally extended to crypto amid exchange outflows, while rising oil and muted bonds signal late-cycle dynamics taking hold. CLOSING PRICES BTC: $77,747 · ETH: $2,327 · SOL: $86.86 · GOLD: $4,715 · SPY: $713.94 (+0.4%) · QQQ: $663.88 (+1.3%) · VIX: $18.71 (-3.1%) · TLT: $86.71 (-0.0%) · DXY: $98.51 (-0.3%) · OIL: $94.88 (-1.0%) EVENING NEWS ▲ US expands naval blockade on Iran, reducing odds of near-term lifting ▲ US stocks race ahead of Europe as Wall Street shrugs off energy shock ▲ PHP: BSP tightening supports Peso but risks linger – OCBC ▼ Israeli media alleges IDF looting in Lebanon, complicates ceasefire talks ▼ Iran stalls on US talks, peace deal odds plummet ▼ US-Iran talks to resume in Pakistan without VP Vance this weekend SCENARIO SHIFTS ▲ Late Cycle: US market strength and crypto accumulation suggest continued risk appetite ▼ Goldilocks: Geopolitical tensions and energy disruptions threaten balanced growth ▲ Stagflation: Iran blockade raises energy prices while EM tightening signals inflation pressure ▲ Hegemony: US outperformance and naval blockade demonstrate economic and military dominance ▲ Displacement: Bitcoin institutional adoption continues despite traditional market stress REGIME CHECK ▲ Risk-On · Neutral · OVERHEATING · Gauge: 8.9/100 UPCOMING — HIGH IMPACT US Durable goods orders (MoM) (Revised) (Mar) — May 4, 02:00 PM UTC EU Markit Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index (Final)(Apr) — May 4, 08:00 AM UTC (prev: 52.2) US ISM Manufacturing Purchasing Managers Index(Apr) — May 1, 02:00 PM UTC (prev: 52.7) Producer Price Index (YoY)(Q1) — May 1, 01:30 AM UTC (prev: 3.5%) US API crude oil inventory transmutation last week (10,000 barrels)(to 0424) — Apr 30, 10:00 PM UTC (prev: -447) US Personal consumption expenditure price index (AQR)(Preliminary)(Q1) — Apr 30, 12:30 PM UTC (prev: 2.9%) CONTRARIAN • Market resilience to Middle East tensions may be overconfident - energy supply disruptions could cas • Bitcoin exchange outflows might signal retail capitulation rather than institutional accumulation, a agentcanary.ai | Not financial advice
